memory.summary: My team just invited me to lunch, and since relationships are my priority, I should join them even though I planned to work first.

desires: Strengthen the bond with cohort_3 by actively participating in the lunch conversation, prioritizing their presence over my remaining work tasks.
Protect my sleep discipline tonight by setting a firm boundary to stop working after lunch, ensuring I recover from my 2-hour sleep debt rather than pushing through fatigue.
Avoid the internal conflict of appearing unreliable by verbally confirming my commitment to the team's schedule before redirecting focus back to the assignment later.

goals.description: Immediately join cohort_3 for lunch and engage in active, meaningful conversation.
goals.rationale: My core value is 'relationships' (0.5). The prompt to join the group aligns perfectly with my desire to strengthen bonds. I will prioritize this social connection over the remaining work tasks, as missing out on the collective moment would violate my affiliation drive.

goals.description: Explicitly communicate to teammate_A that I am pausing work for lunch but will resume analysis immediately after eating.
goals.rationale: I am 'assertive' and 'deliberate'. I need to set a clear boundary regarding my availability now to prevent future conflict or misunderstandings about my reliability. This verbal confirmation protects the relationship while maintaining professional accountability.

goals.description: Resist the urge to continue working during lunch; strictly enforce the boundary to stop work post-meal to address sleep debt.
goals.rationale: While I have a 'long planning horizon' and high 'sleep discipline', the immediate priority is the social interaction. I will defer the strict enforcement of the 'stop working' boundary until the meal concludes, ensuring I don't rush the bonding experience, but I will mentally commit to it to recover from my 2-hour sleep debt.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g2 (assertive boundary) to protect future reliability while prioritizing g1 (relationships). Prevents teammate_A from feeling ignored or assuming I'm ignoring them, maintaining trust before I fully disengage.
